description: Shifts a vector or line forward along its own direction by a specified distance, preserving orientation and magnitude.
utility: Shift a vector forward along its direction by a given distance

arguments.name: vector
arguments.description: The vector or line to shift forward

arguments.name: distance
arguments.description: Distance to shift forward along the vector direction

alternates.description: Shift vector forward by distance along its direction
Shift line forward by distance (returns vector)

examples.scenario: Shift a vector forward by 2 units
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
V = vector(G, O, point(G, 3, 2))
F = forward(G, V, 2)

examples.scenario: Create a stepped progression of vectors
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
V = vector(G, O, point(G, 1, 1))
F1 = forward(G, V, 1)
F2 = forward(G, V, 2)
F3 = forward(G, V, 3)

examples.scenario: Shift a line forward as a vector
examples.expression:
A = point(G, 0, 0)
B = point(G, 4, 0)
L = line(G, A, B)
F = forward(G, L, 3)

P1 = point(G, 1, 1)
P2 = point(G, 4, 3)
V = vector(G, P1, P2)
fwd1 = forward(G, V, 1)
